duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient suspects, without sufficient basis, that others are exploiting, harming, or deceiving them.
The patient is preoccupied with unjustified doubts about the loyalty or trustworthiness of friends or associates.
The patient exhibits a reluctance to confide in others due to an unwarranted fear that the information will be used maliciously against them.
The patient persistently bears grudges and is unforgiving of perceived insults, injuries, or slights.
The patient is quick to react angrily or to counterattack in response to perceived slights or insults.
The patient has recurrent, unjustified suspicions regarding the fidelity of their spouse or sexual partner.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient demonstrates a pervasive pattern of detachment from social relationships and a restricted range of emotional expression.
The patient strongly prefers solitary activities and neither desires nor enjoys close relationships, including being part of a family.
The patient has little, if any, interest in having sexual experiences with another person.
The patient takes pleasure in few, if any, activities.
The patient lacks close friends or confidants other than first-degree relatives.
The patient appears indifferent to the praise or criticism of others.
The patient appears emotionally cold, detached, or demonstrates a flattened affect, seeming indifferent to the praise or criticism of others.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences ideas of reference, interpreting casual incidents and external events as having a particular and unusual meaning specifically for them, excluding actual delusions of reference.
The patient exhibits odd beliefs or magical thinking that influences their behavior and is inconsistent with subcultural norms.
The patient experiences unusual perceptual experiences, including bodily illusions.
The patient displays odd thinking and speech, such as being vague, metaphorical, overelaborate, or stereotyped.
The patient exhibits suspiciousness or paranoid ideation.
The patient exhibits an inappropriate or constricted affect in social and interpersonal situations.
The patient exhibits behavior or an appearance that is odd, eccentric, or peculiar.
The patient lacks close friends or confidants other than first-degree relatives.
The patient experiences excessive social anxiety that does not diminish with familiarity and tends to be associated with paranoid fears rather than negative judgments about self.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 3 or more traits since age 15 + evidence of Conduct Disorder before age 15
duration_rule: Pervasive since age 15; patient is at least 18 years old

symptoms.description: The patient fails to conform to social norms regarding lawful behaviors, repeatedly engaging in acts that are grounds for arrest.
The patient exhibits deceitfulness, as indicated by repeated lying, use of aliases, or conning others for personal profit or pleasure.
The patient exhibits impulsivity or a failure to plan ahead.
The patient exhibits irritability and aggressiveness, as indicated by repeated physical fights or assaults.
The patient exhibits a reckless disregard for the safety of themselves or others.
The patient exhibits consistent irresponsibility, as indicated by a repeated failure to sustain consistent work behavior or honor financial obligations.
The patient lacks remorse, showing indifference to or actively rationalizing having hurt, mistreated, or stolen from another person.
The patient has a history of conduct disorder with an onset before age 15 years.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient makes frantic efforts to avoid real or imagined abandonment.
The patient demonstrates a pattern of unstable and intense interpersonal relationships.
The patient exhibits a markedly and persistently unstable self-image or sense of self.
The patient displays impulsivity in at least two areas that are potentially self-damaging.
The patient engages in recurrent suicidal behavior, gestures, or self-mutilating behavior.
The patient experiences affective instability due to a marked reactivity of mood.
The patient experiences chronic feelings of emptiness or boredom.
The patient exhibits inappropriate, intense anger or has difficulty controlling their anger.
The patient experiences transient, stress-related paranoid ideation or severe dissociative symptoms.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient feels uncomfortable in situations where they are not the center of attention.
The patient exhibits inappropriately sexually seductive or provocative behavior in interactions with others.
The patient displays rapidly shifting and shallow expressions of emotion.
The patient consistently uses their physical appearance to draw attention to themselves.
The patient uses speech that is excessively impressionistic and lacking in detail.
The patient exhibits self-dramatization, theatricality, and exaggerated emotional expression.
The patient is highly suggestible and easily influenced by others or circumstances.
The patient considers relationships to be more intimate than they actually are.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits a grandiose sense of self-importance, exaggerating their achievements and talents.
The patient is preoccupied with fantasies of unlimited success, power, brilliance, beauty, or ideal love.
The patient believes they are special and unique, and can only be understood by, or should associate with, other special or high-status people.
The patient requires excessive admiration from others.
The patient has a strong sense of entitlement, expecting unreasonable favorable treatment or automatic compliance with their expectations.
The patient is interpersonally exploitative, frequently taking advantage of others to achieve their own ends.
The patient lacks empathy and is unwilling to recognize or identify with the feelings and needs of others.
The patient is often envious of others or believes that others are envious of them.
The patient shows arrogant, haughty behaviors or attitudes.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient avoids occupational activities that involve significant interpersonal contact due to fears of criticism, disapproval, or rejection.
The patient is unwilling to get involved with people unless they are certain of being liked.
The patient shows restraint within intimate relationships due to the fear of being shamed or ridiculed.
The patient is overly sensitive to criticism, disapproval, or rejection, leading to profound social inhibition and avoidance of interpersonal contact.
The patient is inhibited in new interpersonal situations because of feelings of inadequacy.
The patient views themselves as socially inept, personally unappealing, or inferior to others.
The patient is unusually reluctant to take personal risks or to engage in any new activities because they may prove embarrassing.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient requires an excessive amount of advice and reassurance from others to make everyday decisions.
The patient needs others to assume responsibility for most major areas of their life.
The patient has difficulty expressing disagreement with others due to an unrealistic fear of losing support or approval.
The patient has difficulty initiating projects or doing things on their own due to a lack of self-confidence rather than a lack of motivation or energy.
The patient goes to excessive lengths to obtain nurturance and support from others, to the point of volunteering to do things that are unpleasant.
The patient feels uncomfortable or helpless when alone due to exaggerated fears of being unable to care for themselves.
The patient urgently seeks another relationship as a source of care and support when a close relationship ends.
The patient is unrealistically preoccupied with fears of being left to care for themselves.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient is preoccupied with details, rules, lists, order, organization, or schedules to the extent that the major point of the activity is lost.
The patient exhibits perfectionism that interferes with task completion.
The patient is excessively devoted to work and productivity to the exclusion of leisure activities and friendships.
The patient is overconscientious, scrupulous, and inflexible about matters of morality, ethics, or values.
The patient is unable to discard worn-out or worthless objects even when they have no sentimental value.
The patient is reluctant to delegate tasks or work with others unless they submit exactly to their way of doing things.
The patient adopts a miserly spending style toward both themselves and others, viewing money as something to be hoarded for future catastrophes.
The patient shows significant rigidity and stubbornness in their attitudes and behaviors.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Personality Change Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Enduring personality disturbance following a physical condition

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits a persistent personality disturbance that is the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
